hypothesis: If a Transformer-based encoder-decoder language model is augmented with a spectral alignment mechanism that jointly learns to reconstruct pure component spectra from mixture IR spectral representations, then it will accurately predict the molecular structures of individual components within IR spectra of mixtures because enforcing spectral reconstruction as an auxiliary training objective constrains the encoder to disentangle overlapping spectral contributions and learn chemically meaningful latent representations that generalize across varying mixture compositions and concentrations.
